name: Niche rejuvenation and support bootstrap for June 2, 2026
summary: Submitted bootstrap bundle establishing the first public baseline candidate for the niche rejuvenation and support track.

scope.question: What is the current human-relevant evidence landscape for Niche Rejuvenation and Support, and what belongs in the first public baseline?

proposed_changes.summary: Add niche rejuvenation and support track linkage to the heterochronic parabiosis study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study directly tests systemic niche influence on aged progenitor repair and should support the new track outlook.

proposed_changes.summary: Add niche rejuvenation and support track linkage to the plasma-dilution study.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study is relevant as systemic-milieu support for aged tissue repair while remaining animal-level evidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add niche rejuvenation and support track linkage to the HSC refractory boundary study.
proposed_changes.rationale: This study constrains tissue-general systemic niche rejuvenation claims.

proposed_changes.summary: Add niche rejuvenation and support track linkage to the young systemic environment finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding anchors systemic-milieu animal support for the first track baseline.

proposed_changes.summary: Add niche rejuvenation and support track linkage to the plasma-dilution animal finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports the systemic-environment component of the new track while preserving its animal-level boundary.

proposed_changes.summary: Add niche rejuvenation and support track linkage to the HSC refractory boundary finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding prevents overbroad claims that bloodborne or systemic niche support works uniformly across aged stem-cell compartments.

proposed_changes.summary: Add oxytocin aged muscle regeneration source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source anchors animal evidence for a declining systemic hormone that supports aged muscle stem-cell activation and regeneration.

proposed_changes.summary: Add fibronectin aged muscle niche source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source supplies direct extracellular-matrix niche-reconstitution evidence for the track.

proposed_changes.summary: Add NF-kB myofiber satellite-cell niche source.
proposed_changes.rationale: This source captures cell-non-autonomous inflammatory niche support and a pharmacological aged-mouse repair signal.

proposed_changes.summary: Add oxytocin aged mouse muscle regeneration study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record maps systemic hormone support to aged muscle stem-cell activation and repair endpoints.

proposed_changes.summary: Add fibronectin aged muscle niche reconstitution study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record maps extracellular-matrix niche support to aged muscle stem-cell and regeneration endpoints.

proposed_changes.summary: Add NF-kB myofiber satellite-cell niche study record.
proposed_changes.rationale: The study record maps inflammatory cell-non-autonomous myofiber signaling to satellite-cell niche and aged repair endpoints.

proposed_changes.summary: Add oxytocin aged muscle stem-cell activation finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports animal_signal by showing a systemic hormone can improve aged muscle stem-cell activation and repair in mice.
This finding provides direct animal evidence that restoring an aged extracellular-matrix niche component can improve regeneration.

proposed_changes.summary: Add NF-kB inhibition aged muscle repair finding.
proposed_changes.rationale: This finding supports animal_signal for a cell-non-autonomous inflammatory niche mechanism.

proposed_changes.summary: Create the first public outlook for the Niche Rejuvenation and Support track.
proposed_changes.rationale: A low-confidence animal_signal baseline is justified by multiple mouse niche-support and systemic-milieu findings, while human niche-engagement evidence remains absent.
uncertainty_flags: Systemic milieu, endocrine, extracellular-matrix, and inflammatory niche mechanisms may not translate as one unified intervention class.
Human plasma-exchange biomarker context is adjacent but does not measure stem-cell niche engagement.
Overlap with circulating-factor modulation and stem-cell rejuvenation requires review.

proposed_outlook_implications.note: Create the first public track outlook at animal_signal with steady momentum and low confidence because mouse systemic-milieu, hormone, ECM, and inflammatory-niche interventions support repair while human niche-engagement evidence is absent.
Publishing this bundle would broaden stem-cell exhaustion coverage beyond cell-intrinsic rejuvenation and replacement/transplantation with a niche-support baseline.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
